Farn PolrrrcAl- Pnncrrcss CoMMrssroN
428 J Street o Suite (r20 o Sacrarnento, CA 95}14-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
September 2,2010
Brant M. Miller
7 Jackson Court
Novato, CA94647-4432
Re:
Yourr Request for Advice
Our File No. A-10-129
Dear Mr. Miller:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the reporting provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").' Our advice is based on the facts prisent"d.- th" Fair political
Practices Commission (the "Comrnission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders
advice. (ln re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.)
QUESTIONS
l. As a retiree of the Chevron Corporation and potential member of the Marin County
Grand Jury, must your interest in approximately $15,000 of Chevron stock be disclosed on you,
statement of economic interests'l
2. As a retiree of the Chevron Corporation and potential member of the Marin County
Grand Jury, must payments by Chevron for retirement benefits, limited to a life insurance poii"y
and retiree health care benefits, be disclosed on your statement of economic interests?
3. As a potential member of the Marin County Grand Jury, must your interest in your
personal residence be disclosed on your statement of economic interests?
CONCLUSIONS
l. Your stock in the Chevron Corporation is an investment in a business entity and must
be disclosed as required by the Marin County Grand Jury's conflict-of-interest cocle.
' 'rhe Political Retirrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections 8 I000 through g l0l4. All statutory
ret'erencesaret<ltheGtlvernmentCocle,unlessotherwiseindicated. Theregulationsofthef'"airpotitical practices
CommissionarecontainedinSectionsl8ll0throughlsggTofTitle2olthecalitbrniaCodeofRegulations. All
regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless prherwise in<ticated.


File No. A-10-129
Page No. 2
2. Payments by Chevron for a life insurance policy and retiree health care benefits are
considered income and must be disclosed as required by the Marin County Grand Jury's conflict-
of-interest code, unless the payments are made under a defined benefit pension plan qualified
under Internal Revenue Code Section 401(a).
3' Your personal residence is not considered an interest in real property for purpose of
reporting. Thus, you are not required to disclose this interest under the Marin Couniy Grand
Jury' s confl ict-of-interest code.
FACTS
You are an alternate for service on the 2010-2011 Marin County Grand Jury. If seated,
you will be required to file a Statement of Economic Interests (Form 700) as requiied by the
Marin County Grand Jury's conflict-of-interest code. As adopted in 2005, the Marin County
Grand Jury's conflict-of-interest code incorporates by reference Regulation 18730. More
specifically, the code also requires members of the grand jury to disclose the following:
"All investments, and business positions in business entities, and income
from sources, which have done business with, or persons who were employees of,
the county or any city, town, joint powers agency or any special purpoi"
assessing or taxing district located wholly or partly in the County, within the
previotts two years, and any interests in real property within the County."
In light of the Marin County Grand Jury's conflict-of-interest code, you seek assistance
regarding the Act's reporting requirements for the following specific interejts:
o 200 shares of chevron stock with an estimated value of $15,000.
o Payments by chevron for retiree benefits including payments of $950 per year
for a life insurance policy and payments of $2,100 per year for retiree treatttr
care benefits.
o Your personal residence located within the County of Marin.
YoLl state that you are retired and no longer work for the Chevron Corporation.
Other than payments for the life insurance policy and retiree health care benefits, you
receive no other payments from the corporation. Additionally, it is your understanding
that Chevron either sells, directly or indirectly, bulk product to either the County of
Marin or local agencies within the county. At a minimum, Chevron competes with other
companies for contracts to sell gasoline to either the County of Marin or local agencies
within the county.


File No. A-10-129
Page No. 3
ANALYSIS
One of the stated purposes of the Act is to ensure that public officials "perform their
duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or the
financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 81001(b).) Section 81002(c)
provides that the assets and income of public officials that may be materially affected by their
official actions should be disclosed. To implement these provisions, the Act requires certain
public officials to file statements of economic interests disclosing their financial interests.
(Sections 87200, 87300, and 87302.)
Should you become a member of the Marin County Grand Jury you are subject to the
grand jury's conflict-of-interest code. As applicable to your particular circumstances, the Marin
County Grand Jury's conflict-of-interest code specifically requires that you report all
investments in, and income from, business entities that have done business with the county, or
any local governmental agency within the county, in the previous two years and any interests in
real property within the county. Clarifying the Marin County Grand Jury's conf'lict-of-interest
code the Act further defines those investment interests, interests in sources of income, and
interests in real properties that must be disclosed.
Stock Ownership
Section 82034 defines the term "investment" in pertinent part as the following:
"[A]ny financial interest in or security issued by a business entity,
including, but not limited to, common stock, preferred stock, rights, warrants,
options, debt instmments, and any partnership or other ownership interest owned
directly, indirectly, or beneficially by the public official, or other filer, or his or
her immediate family. . .."
A business entity is considered an investment under Section 82034 only "if the business
entity or any parent, subsidiary, or otherwise related business entity has an interest in real
property in the jurisdiction, or does business or plans to do business in the jurisdiction, or has
done business in the jurisdiction" in the previou.s two years. Adclitionally,"an asset shall not be
deemed an investment unless its fair market value equals or exceeds $2,000.
You currently own approximately $15,000 in Chevron stock. You have also stated that
Chevron conducts business with the county or local agencies within the county, or at least
competes to do so with other gasoline companies. Therefore, you have an investment in
Chevron as defined by Section 82034. Moreover, under the Marin County Grand Jury's conflict-
of-interest code, you must report all investments in business entities that have done business with
the county or any city, town, joint powers agency or any special purpose assessing or taxing
district located wholly or partly in the county, within the previous two years. Thus, based ipon


File No. A-10-129
Page No. 4
the facts you have provided you have a reportable investment interest in Chevron that must be
reported under the Marin county Grand Jury's conflict-of-interest code.2
Retiree Benefits
In pertinent part, Section 82030 defines income as any payment received including, but
not limited to, the following:
"[A]ny salary, wage, advance, dividend, interest, rent, proceeds from any
sale, gift, including any gift of food or beverage, loan, forgiveness or payment of
indebtedness received by the filer, reimbursement for expenses, per diem, or
contribution to an insurance or pension program paid by any p"rio, other than an
employer, and including any community property interest in ihe income of a
spouse."
For purposes of an initial assuming office statement, income is reportable if it aggregates
to $500 or more in value from any particular sollrce within the 12 months prior to u*u*ing
office. Income must be subsequently reported on annual statements of economic interests if it
aggregates to $500 or more in value from any particular source and is received in the calendar
year covered by the statement or, if in the same calendar year, since the date of assuming office.
(Sections 87207 and 87302.)
Under the Marin County Grand Jury's conflict-of-interest code, you must report all
income from business entities that have done business with the county oi urry city, town, joint
powers agency or any special purpose assessing or taxing district located wholly or partli in the
county, within the previous two years. As you have provided the fact that Chevron conducts
business with the county or local agencies within the county, or at least competes to do so with
other gasoline companies, your income from Chevron, even in the form of payments for a life
insurance policy and retiree health care benefits, is reportable unless an exception applies.3
The Act enumerates several exceptions to the definition of the term "income.,, One
exception that may be applicable to your account of the facts is the Act's exception for payments
received under a defined benefit pension plan qualified under Internal Revenue Code Section
401(a). (Section 82030(bX11).) While you have stated that Chevron does not provide you a
pension other than payments for a life insurance policy and payments for retiree health care
benefits, you have not specified whether either of these payments may fall under a defined
benefit pension plan qualified under Internal Revenue Code Sectio, +Ot1o;. you may want to
consult a tax advisor regarding Internal Revenue Code Section 401(a). If either payment is
received under a defined benefit payment pension plan qualified under Internal Revenue Code
I This interest must be reported on Schedule A- I of your Form 700.
r If reportable, these payments must be reported on schedule c of your Form 700.


File No. A-10-129
Page No.5
Section 401(a), the payment is not considered income under the Act and need not be disclosed
under the Marin County Grand Jury's conflict-of-interest code.
Residence
Section 82033 defines an "interest in real property" to include the following:
"[Alny leasehold, beneficial or ownership interest or an option to acquire
such an interest in real property located in the jurisdiction owned directly,
indirectly or beneficially by the public official, or other filer, or his or her
immediate family if the fair market value of the interest is two thousand dollars
($2,000) or more."
Notwithstanding this definition, Section 87206 specifically states that an "'interest in real
property' does not include the principal residence of the filer or any other property which the
filer utilizes as the personal residence of the filer" for purposes of disclosui..4 Thur, you,
interest in your personal residence is not a reportable interest in real property under the Marin
County Grand Jury's conflict-of-interest code.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916\ 322-5660
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division
'' Note that Section 87206 excludes personal residences fiom the det'inition of "interest in real property,,
only lor purposes of the Act's disclosure provisions. For purposes of governmental decision-making, the Act's
contlict-ot--interest provision prohibit an official tiom making, participating in making, or intiuencirig any
governmerital decision with a reasonably tbreseeable material eft'ect on an economic interest in real froperty,
including an economic interest in a personal residence.

QUESTION: l. As a retiree of the Chevron Corporation and potential member of the Marin County
Grand Jury, must your interest in approximately $15,000 of Chevron stock be disclosed on you,
statement of economic interests'l
2. As a retiree of the Chevron Corporation and potential member of the Marin County
Grand Jury, must payments by Chevron for retirement benefits, limited to a life insurance poii"y
and retiree health care benefits, be disclosed on your statement of economic interests?
3. As a potential member of the Marin County Grand Jury, must your interest in your
personal residence be disclosed on your statement of economic interests?

CONCLUSION: l. Your stock in the Chevron Corporation is an investment in a business entity and must
be disclosed as required by the Marin County Grand Jury's conflict-of-interest cocle.
